engineIntelligence.h2: Alfa Romeo Giulia Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Alfa Romeo Giulia in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Alfa Romeo Giulia 55273835 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger failure - common between 40,000-60,000 miles causing loss of power and boost issues.
Timing chain stretch - rattling noise on cold start, may require full engine replacement if neglected.

cards.altText: Alfa Romeo Giulia 55282368 engine - diesel 2.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Balance shaft bolt failure - pre-2019 units prone to catastrophic failure requiring full engine replacement.
DPF/AdBlue system faults - regeneration issues and pump failures common on high-mileage examples.

cards.altText: Alfa Romeo Giulia 67005023 engine - petrol 2.9L V6 replacement UK specialist
knownFailures: Fuel injector failure - high-pressure injectors can fail causing misfires and rough running.
Oil consumption issues - some units report elevated oil use requiring monitoring between services.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £6,000-£12,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 2.9 V6 units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Alfa Romeo Giulia Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Alfa Romeo Giulia's 2.0 Turbo petrol and 2.2 JTDm diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain, turbocharger and balance shaft bolt failures most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Balance Shaft Bolt Failure - Why Pre-2019 Giulia Diesel Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Alfa Romeo Giulia Super, Veloce, Speciale 2.2 JTDm (2016-2019, codes 55282368, 55282714)
problems.rootCause: Factory balance shaft retaining bolts can loosen on pre-2019 JTDm engines, causing catastrophic bearing damage and engine seizure if not addressed.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, inspect/replace balance shaft bolts, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before bearing damage; not suitable post-failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt JTDm unit with revised bolts, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended post-failure; addresses root cause with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: On pre-2019 Giulia diesels over 50k miles, verify balance shaft bolt condition before purchase. If failure has occurred, replacement with a rebuilt unit featuring revised bolts offers better long-term value than attempting repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.2 JTDm engine prices for your Alfa Romeo Giulia

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why Giulia 2.0 Turbo Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Alfa Romeo Giulia Super, Veloce, Speciale 2.0 Turbo (2016-2024, codes 55273835, 55278583)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearing wear and seal failure on high-mileage 2.0 Turbo units causes oil leakage, loss of boost, and excessive smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old turbo, fit reconditioned unit, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine block is undamaged; may not address secondary wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2.0 Turbo with upgraded turbo, full gasket set, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value on higher-mileage cars where turbo failure indicates broader wear

problems.recommendation: If your Giulia 2.0 Turbo shows turbo failure symptoms under 50k miles, a standalone turbo replacement may suffice. Above 60k miles or with oil contamination, a full rebuilt engine often provides better reliability and value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 2.0 Turbo Alfa Romeo Giulia engines

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure - Why Giulia 2.0 Turbo Engines Rattle on Cold Start
problems.affectedModels: Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.0 Turbo variants (2016-2024, codes 55273835, 55278583)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain stretch, causing rattling on cold start and potential valve timing loss if neglected.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before valve/piston damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2.0 Turbo with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if rattling accompanied by misfires or loss of compression

problems.recommendation: A cold-start rattle on your Giulia 2.0 Turbo warrants immediate inspection. If chain stretch is confirmed but no internal damage exists, preventative replacement is cost-effective. If valves are affected, replacement is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare timing chain repair vs replacement costs for your Alfa Romeo Giulia

problems.h4: DPF & AdBlue System Failure - Why Giulia Diesel Engines Trigger Emissions Warnings
problems.affectedModels: Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.2 JTDm variants (2016-2021, codes 55282368, 55282714)
problems.rootCause: DPF clogging from short-journey driving and AdBlue pump/injector faults cause regeneration failure, limp mode, and costly emissions system repairs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Forced DPF regeneration, AdBlue pump/injector replacement, system reset
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine mechanicals are sound; may not prevent recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt JTDm with updated emissions components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if DPF failure coincides with turbo or balance shaft issues

problems.recommendation: Giulia diesels used primarily for short trips are prone to DPF issues. If your car shows repeated regeneration failures alongside other mechanical symptoms, a rebuilt engine with updated emissions hardware offers a more reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 2.2 JTDm engines with updated emissions systems

problems.h4: Start-Stop System Malfunction - Why Giulia Engines Show Battery/Charging Warnings
problems.affectedModels: All Alfa Romeo Giulia variants with start-stop (2016-2024)
problems.rootCause: AGM battery degradation and alternator control module faults cause start-stop system errors, warning lights, and no-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace AGM battery, reset BMS, test alternator output
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated electrical faults without engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine if start-stop failure caused repeated cranking damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only recommended if electrical faults led to mechanical wear or no-start damage

problems.recommendation: Start-stop warnings on your Giulia are usually electrical, not mechanical. Replace the AGM battery and reset the system first. Only consider engine replacement if repeated no-start events caused internal damage.
problems.cta: Compare electrical repair vs engine replacement quotes for your Alfa Romeo Giulia

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Alfa Romeo Giulia
finalCta.paragraph: From balance shaft bolts on pre-2019 diesels to turbocharger wear on 2.0 Turbo petrols, the Alfa Romeo Giulia's most common engine failures are well-documented. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Alfa Romeo Giulia specialists who understand the Giorgio platform, ZF 8-speed integration, and Ferrari-derived V6 complexity. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Alfa Romeo Giulia Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Alfa Romeo Giulia and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Alfa Romeo Giulia Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Alfa Romeo Giulia is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

groups.title: Alfa Romeo Giulia Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L JTDm diesel powered the Giulia from launch through 2021, offered in 160hp and 190hp tunes across Super, Speciale and Veloce trims. This efficient unit delivers strong torque for motorway cruising but requires careful maintenance of its emissions systems.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft bolt failure on pre-2019 units; DPF/AdBlue system faults on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.2L diesel engines

groups.title: Alfa Romeo Giulia Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L GME T4 petrol engine was the Giulia's volume seller, available in 200hp and 280hp outputs. Paired with ZF's 8-speed auto and rear-wheel drive, it delivers engaging dynamics but requires attention to turbocharger and timing chain health on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger wear at 40k-60k miles; timing chain stretch causing cold-start rattle
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Ferrari-derived 2.9L V6 Bi-Turbo is exclusive to Quadrifoglio performance models, producing 510hp. This high-strung unit demands specialist knowledge for replacement, with particular attention to fuel injection system integrity and oil consumption monitoring on track-used examples.
commonFailurePoints: High-pressure injector failure; elevated oil consumption on heavily driven examples
items.cta: Enquire about Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.9L V6 engine availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Alfa Romeo Giulia needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Alfa Romeo Giulia specialists.

engineYears.h2: Alfa Romeo Giulia Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Alfa Romeo Giulia's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: GME T4 Petrol & JTDm Diesel Debut
years.description: The Giulia launched with 2.0L GME T4 petrol (200/280hp) and 2.2L JTDm diesel (160/190hp) engines on the new Giorgio platform.
keyChanges: Giulia Type 952 platform launched with rear-wheel drive and ZF 8-speed auto
2.0L GME T4 petrol introduced in 200hp and 280hp outputs
2.2L JTDm diesel offered in 160hp and 190hp tunes
Quadrifoglio variant launched with Ferrari-derived 2.9L V6 Bi-Turbo
knownFor: Pre-2019 JTDm diesel balance shaft bolt recall requiring verification
Early 2.0 Turbo turbocharger wear patterns emerging at 40k-60k miles
Quadrifoglio V6 demands specialist knowledge and premium pricing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2018 Alfa Romeo Giulia replacement engines
years.ticker: Giulia 2.2 diesel balance shaft bolt • Giulia 2.0 Turbo turbo failure • Quadrifoglio V6 injector issues

years.preview: Mid-Life Updates: Revised Diesel & Facelift Prep
years.description: 2019+ JTDm diesel engines received revised balance shaft bolts; petrol range continued with minor updates ahead of 2023 facelift.
keyChanges: Revised balance shaft bolts fitted to 2.2L JTDm from 2019 production
Minor ECU updates for emissions compliance across petrol/diesel range
Veloce Ti trim introduced with enhanced equipment
popularModels: Alfa Romeo Giulia Veloce Ti 2.0 Turbo
knownFor: Post-2019 JTDm units less prone to balance shaft failure but still require DPF maintenance
2.0 Turbo timing chain wear remains a consideration above 50k miles
Quadrifoglio NRING limited edition commands premium replacement pricing
years.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo Giulia 2019-2021 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Giulia diesel DPF regeneration • 2.0 Turbo timing chain rattle • Quadrifoglio oil consumption monitoring

years.description: Diesel engines discontinued in UK from 2022; 2023 facelift introduced updated 2.0L petrol with 276hp output.
keyChanges: Diesel engines discontinued for UK market from 2022 model year
2023 facelift Giulia introduced with revised 2.0L petrol producing 276hp
Quadrifoglio continues with 2.9L V6 Bi-Turbo, minor equipment updates
knownFor: Facelift 2.0L petrol benefits from minor revisions but shares core architecture with pre-facelift units
Quadrifoglio V6 remains unchanged, requiring same specialist replacement approach
Used pre-2022 diesel variants still in circulation require careful engine code verification
years.cta: Enquire about Alfa Romeo Giulia 2022-2024 engine availability

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Alfa Romeo Giulia was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Alfa Romeo Giulia specialists.

faq.h2: Alfa Romeo Giulia Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace an Alfa Romeo Giulia engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Alfa Romeo Giulia engine costs range from £2,500 for a 2.0L petrol 200hp unit to £12,000+ for the Quadrifoglio 2.9L V6, depending on variant, engine code and supplier. Supply-only pricing excludes fitting labour.
keyPoints: 2.0L petrol 200hp rebuilt: £2,500-£2,900 supply only
2.0L petrol 280hp rebuilt: £2,800-£3,500 supply only
2.2L diesel rebuilt: £2,800-£4,000 supply only
Quadrifoglio V6 rebuilt: £6,000-£12,000+ supply only
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Alfa Romeo Giulia engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.2 diesel engine reliable?
items.answer: Post-2019 Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.2 JTDm diesel engines are generally reliable with revised balance shaft bolts, but pre-2019 units require verification before purchase due to known bolt failure risk.
keyPoints: Pre-2019 JTDm: verify balance shaft bolt condition before fitting
Post-2019 JTDm: revised bolts reduce catastrophic failure risk
DPF/AdBlue maintenance critical for long-term diesel reliability
items.cta: Get quotes for revised-bolt 2.2 JTDm Alfa Romeo Giulia engines

items.question: What causes turbo failure on the Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.0 Turbo?
items.answer: Turbocharger failure on Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.0 Turbo engines typically occurs between 40,000-60,000 miles due to bearing wear and seal degradation, causing loss of boost and oil consumption.
keyPoints: Common failure mileage: 40k-60k miles on high-use examples
Symptoms: loss of power, excessive smoke, rattling under boost
Replacement cost: £1,200-£2,300 standalone; £2,500-£3,500 full rebuilt engine
items.cta: Compare turbo repair vs full engine replacement quotes for your Giulia

items.question: Can I fit a Stelvio engine in my Alfa Romeo Giulia?
items.answer: Yes, Alfa Romeo Giulia and Stelvio share the same Giorgio platform and engine codes (2.0L GME T4, 2.2L JTDm, 2.9L V6), making cross-model engine swaps mechanically compatible with proper ECU coding.
keyPoints: Same engine codes: 55273835, 55282368, 67005023 fit both models
ZF 8HP50 gearbox coding required after engine swap
Ensure donor engine matches your variant's emissions specification
items.cta: Verify engine code compatibility before ordering your Alfa Romeo Giulia replacement

items.question: How long does an Alfa Romeo Giulia engine replacement take?
items.answer: Alfa Romeo Giulia engine replacement typically takes 2-4 days for supply & fit by a specialist, depending on variant complexity, parts availability and whether ZF gearbox coding is required.
keyPoints: Supply-only engines: dispatch within 24-48 hours from UK stock
Supply & fit: 2-4 days including coding and road test
Quadrifoglio V6 may require longer due to specialist component sourcing
items.cta: Find Alfa Romeo Giulia engine supply & fit specialists with fast turnaround

items.question: Do you offer warranty on Alfa Romeo Giulia replacement engines?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt or reconditioned Alfa Romeo Giulia engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with extended options available for Quadrifoglio units.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour when using network supply & fit specialists
Quadrifoglio V6 units may qualify for extended 24-month coverage
items.cta: Compare warranty terms when requesting your Alfa Romeo Giulia engine quotes

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on an Alfa Romeo Giulia?
items.answer: Replacing the engine on an Alfa Romeo Giulia is often worthwhile given the car's strong residual values and engaging driving dynamics, especially for Veloce and Quadrifoglio variants with low overall mileage.
keyPoints: Giulia retains strong value; engine replacement can extend life cost-effectively
Rebuilt 2.0L petrol from £2,500 often cheaper than vehicle depreciation
Quadrifoglio V6 replacement preserves high-performance asset value
items.cta: Get a tailored quote to assess repair vs replace value for your specific Giulia

items.question: What engine codes fit the Alfa Romeo Giulia?
items.answer: Alfa Romeo Giulia compatible engine codes include 55273835 (2.0L 280hp), 55278583 (2.0L 200hp), 55282368/55282714 (2.2L JTDm diesel), and 67005023 (2.9L V6 Quadrifoglio).
keyPoints: Diesel: 55282368 (160hp), 55282714 (190hp) - verify pre/post-2019 bolt revision
Always confirm engine code matches your variant's ECU and emissions spec
items.cta: Use our registration lookup to identify your exact Alfa Romeo Giulia engine code

items.question: Where can I find used Alfa Romeo Giulia engines for sale?
items.answer: Used Alfa Romeo Giulia engines are available through EngineMarket's network of UK breakers and specialists, with prices from £1,800 for 2.0L petrol units to £8,500+ for Quadrifoglio V6 donors.
keyPoints: Request compression test and service history before purchasing used
Verify balance shaft bolt status on pre-2019 diesel donor engines
Budget for ancillary replacement (mounts, hoses) during installation
items.cta: Check used Alfa Romeo Giulia engine availability with warranty options

items.question: Do you supply and fit Alfa Romeo Giulia engines?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with UK Alfa Romeo Giulia specialists offering supply & fit engine replacement, including ZF 8-speed coding, carbon prop shaft handling and variant-specific ECU programming.
keyPoints: Supply & fit includes removal, fitting, coding and road test
Specialists trained on Giorgio platform and Ferrari-derived V6 systems
Labour costs typically £800-£2,500 added to engine price
